_metadata.description: Other/inactive ingredients database for supplement excipients and additives

functional_roles_field.purpose: Multi-valued functional role(s) for the Flutter UI tap-to-learn chip layer. Display-only — no scoring impact in V1. May be empty in V1 entries; populated in Phase 3 backfill batches per scripts/audits/functional_roles/. Final 100% completeness enforced by coverage_gate.py in Phase 5.

_metadata.additive_type_field_status: DEPRECATED (Phase 4) — to be dropped after backfill batches complete. Replaced by functional_roles[] which expresses multi-valued roles correctly. Do not add new values to additive_type going forward.

v5.2.0.is_label_descriptor: Phase 4a flag (2026-04-30): true on label-noise entries (marketing_descriptor / phytochemical_marker / source_descriptor / etc.). Suppressed from Flutter inactive_ingredients[] blob.
v5.2.0.is_active_only: Phase 4a flag (2026-04-30): true on entries that will relocate to the active-ingredient pipeline in V1.1 (botanical_extract, glandular_tissue, branded complexes, amino_acid_derivative, phytocannabinoids, marine extracts). Suppressed from inactive_ingredients[].

field_contract_changes.v5.3.0: 2026-04-30 Phase 4c — category canonicalization. Renamed 241 → ~30 values per CLINICIAN_REVIEW.md Section 2B. Each category now equals either a functional_roles vocab ID or a transitional bucket (label_descriptor / active_pending_relocation / manual_review). No Flutter impact — Flutter reads functional_roles[], not category.
field_contract_changes.v5.4.0: 2026-04-30 Phase 6 — V1.1 attributes scaffolding. Added attributes object with is_branded_complex on heuristic-detected branded ingredient complexes (BioCell, Tonalin, AstraGin, etc.) — those flagged is_active_only AND with brand-marker name patterns.

other_ingredients.cui_note: UNII 331KBJ17RK (CANOLA OIL) removed 2026-05-14 — this is a pure source-descriptor entry for generic 'Canola' label disclosures, NOT for canola oil specifically. FDA cache contains no generic 'canola' UNII (only canola oil 331KBJ17RK and Brassica napus whole NFP6814VZ4). Tagging the descriptor with canola oil's UNII would force every generic 'Canola' label disclosure to UNII-match as the oil. RxCUI 1309202 (Canola oil) preserved for downstream reference but UNII intentionally absent. Specific canola products (PII_CANOLA_OIL, NHA_CANOLA_LECITHIN, etc.) keep their own UNIIs.

Generic capsule delivery format descriptor. Not a specific ingredient but a formulation type. Classified as non-active to prevent unmapped inflation. UNII 3NXW29V3WO (hypromelloses/HPMC) was removed 2026-05-14 — generic capsules can be gelatin, HPMC, pullulan, etc., so a single specific UNII would mis-anchor every generic-capsule disclosure to HPMC. Specific capsule materials are modeled in separate entries (PII_HPMC, PII_GELATIN_CAPSULE, etc.).
other_ingredients.cui_note: UNII intentionally omitted — generic capsule is a delivery-format class (gelatin / HPMC / pullulan / etc.), no single FDA-cataloged substance covers the class. Specific capsule materials carry their own UNIIs in dedicated entries.

Highly Branched Cyclic Dextrin (HBCD) — Glico-trademarked 'Cluster Dextrin' is the most prominent commercial form. Produced from waxy maize starch by 1,4-alpha-glucan branching enzyme treatment yielding a high-molecular-weight (typical avg MW ~160k Da) cyclic glucan with low osmolality. Marketed for sports/endurance nutrition (rapid gastric emptying vs. maltodextrin). Distinct from generic Modified Food Starch and from ModCarb (Lonza-branded multi-source dextrin blend, NHA_MODCARB). No single-compound CAS, PubChem CID, or UNII exists — HBCD is a polymer product class, not a single chemical entity. Generic parent class: Dextrin (NHA_DEXTRIN, CUI C0054527). UMLS verification 2026-05-01: no concept exists for 'highly branched cyclic dextrin', 'cluster dextrin', or 'branched dextrin' (verify_cui.py).
other_ingredients.cui_note: Polymer product class with no single UMLS concept. Generic dextrin parent CUI C0054527 covers the chemical family but does not represent the highly-branched cyclic structural form.

other_ingredients.notes: Multi-element trace mineral product (typical brands: ConcenTrace, Trace Minerals Research, plus 'Ocean-derived'/'Clay-Derived' formulations). These products list 50-70+ elements at trace ppb-to-low-ppm levels — including lanthanides (Lanthanum, Cerium, Praseodymium, Neodymium, Samarium, Europium, Gadolinium, Terbium, Dysprosium, Holmium, Erbium, Thulium, Ytterbium, Lutetium, Yttrium, Scandium) and other rare/heavy elements (Barium, Beryllium, Cesium, Gallium, Hafnium, Indium, Iridium, Niobium, Palladium, Platinum, Gold, Rhenium, Rhodium, Ruthenium, Tantalum, Tellurium, Thallium, Thorium, Titanium, Tungsten, Uranium, Zirconium). EXECUTIVE DECISION 2026-05-01 (Option A): treat as a single non-bioactive descriptor entry rather than scoring each element individually. Rationale: (1) elements are at trace levels with no nutritional bioavailability scoring relevance; (2) per-element entries would explode complexity without scoring value; (3) toxic-at-high-dose elements (Pb, Hg, Cd, Tl, U) at ppb levels are considered safe per existing FDA guidance for these products; (4) avoids false penalties on legitimately-marketed trace mineral products. Sulfur and elemental Sulphur listings are NOT covered here — those should map to a proper sulfur entry if/when added (sulfur is nutritionally relevant via cysteine/methionine).
other_ingredients.cui_note: Multi-element source product; no single UMLS concept covers the heterogeneous element list. Generic ascription per Executive Option A 2026-05-01 (single trace_mineral_solution entry rather than per-element entries).

other_ingredients.notes: Resolvin D5 — D-series resolvin, a Specialized Pro-resolving Mediator (SPM) derived from DHA via the 17-HDHA pathway. Distinct chemical entity from its 17-HDHA precursor (CUI C0639949). Endogenously short-lived; commercial supplements rarely contain measurable preformed Resolvin D5 — most 'SPM' products deliver the precursor (HDHA) which the body converts. Listed in other_ingredients (not IQM) per 2026-05-01 IQM-purity rule: no verified clinical bio_score data exists for oral preformed Resolvin D5 supplementation. Identity verified (UMLS C3492734 + PubChem CID 24932575). When/if clinical PK + efficacy data emerge, can be promoted to IQM.
other_ingredients.clinical_notes: Identity verified. Clinical role pending — see CLINICIAN_REVIEW_REQUEST_2026-05-01.md.

other_ingredients.notes: Protectin DX (PDX) — DHA-derived double-lipoxygenase Specialized Pro-resolving Mediator (SPM). Stereoisomer-distinct from Protectin D1; biologically less active per published mediator-lipidomics data but still a distinct chemical entity. Endogenously short-lived; commercial supplements rarely contain measurable preformed Protectin DX. Listed in other_ingredients (not IQM) per 2026-05-01 IQM-purity rule: no verified clinical bio_score data exists for oral preformed Protectin DX supplementation. Identity verified (UMLS C3886642 + PubChem CID 11968800).
other_ingredients.clinical_notes: Identity verified. Clinical role pending — see CLINICIAN_REVIEW_REQUEST_2026-05-01.md.

other_ingredients.notes: Generic algae protein listing where the source is not specified (Spirulina, Chlorella, Schizochytrium, mixed microalgae). Listed in other_ingredients (not IQM) per 2026-05-01 IQM-purity rule + clinician 'no strong scoring weight' policy: this entry is for label-identity only, NOT for protein-quality scoring. When source IS disclosed, the species-specific IQM entries (chlorella, spirulina) handle scoring with their verified bio_scores. Chlorella whole-cell has documented near-zero amino acid bioavailability (PMID:39610880); spirulina protein digestibility ~70-80% but cell-wall barrier still applies.
other_ingredients.cui_note: C0600607 = generic 'Algal Proteins' MSH concept. Source-specific entries (chlorella, spirulina) carry their own scoring; this descriptor entry routes label-only generic listings.
other_ingredients.clinical_notes: Generic descriptor. Source-specific entries should be preferred when label discloses the algal species.

High Amylopectin Starch — sports-nutrition carbohydrate fuel derived from waxy maize (corn) starch, which is naturally >95% amylopectin. DSLD ships products like GNC Wheybolic with the FDA UNII TQI9LJM246 (AMYLOPECTIN, CORN) on this active-panel row at 250–500 mg per serving, with source-form 'Waxy Maize'. Same product family as Highly Branched Cyclic Dextrin (NHA_HBCD_CLUSTER_DEXTRIN); HBCD is the enzyme-treated derivative of this precursor. Marketed for fast gastric emptying / rapid post-workout glycogen replenishment vs. maltodextrin or sucrose. Recognized as an active fuel ingredient but not scored for therapeutic quality (no premium-vs-lower form distinction). UNII verified 2026-05-14 against fda_unii_cache.json: TQI9LJM246 → 'AMYLOPECTIN, CORN'. Generic unspecified-source UNII 4XO4QFV777 (AMYLOPECTIN, UNSPECIFIED SOURCE) and per-source UNIIs (wheat BZ5AL1C3TJ, rice FCF4HG24O1, potato CDT7MX9YQ5, tapioca KL9H9HAD19, pea RQ1DI8911W, oat 1LE22FEY4Z) exist for other source forms.
